response.justification: Mohammed has 15+ years of broad software engineering experience and genuine staff-level depth in TypeScript/Node.js, but he does not meet the core requirement of 8+ years building production-grade AI solutions. His AI-specific work appears to have begun in April 2025 with his current role, where it is mentioned only briefly alongside other responsibilities. Python—the primary language for AI/ML work in this domain—does not appear in his skills. There is no evidence of experience with vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM evaluation pipelines, or multi-agent frameworks, which are all either required or strongly preferred for this role.
strengths: Strong TypeScript and Node.js depth across multiple production environments, directly matching one of the two primary languages listed
Exposure to MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers in his current role aligns with a specific key technology called out in the job description
Demonstrated staff-level engineering breadth including high-throughput data integration (Dunnhumby, millions of HTTP req/s) and AWS CDK-based cloud infrastructure (Optilyz)
concerns: No evidence of 8 years of AI/ML production experience; meaningful AI work appears to have started only in April 2025 and is described in one brief line
Python is absent from his listed skills and work history, despite being the dominant language for AI engineering and a primary requirement for this role
No mention of vector databases, retrieval-augmented generation, LLM performance optimization, or multi-agent frameworks such as LangGraph or CrewAI

key_factors.factor: Missing Python, RAG, and vector DB skills
